# 1. Executive Snapshot

- **The Agentic AI race has a new apex model.** Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9 — the first generally available Mythos-class model. Access was briefly suspended June 12 due to demand overload; this signals capacity strain at the frontier, an infrastructure risk for any company depending on these APIs without fallback planning.

- **Anthropic is now the most valuable private company on Earth** at a $965B valuation ($65B Series H, May 28), with $47B annualized run-rate revenue. Claude Code is the single largest driver of Supabase's 600% database growth. The AI coding agent ecosystem is no longer a trend — it is the default engineering paradigm.

- **Physical AI crosses from pilots to platforms.** Jeff Bezos's Prometheus raised $12B at a $41B valuation to build an "artificial general engineer." NEURA Robotics closed $1.4B Series C. Figure AI signed its first retail distribution deal. Humanoid robots are entering commercial production and supply chains now.

- **GitHub Agentic Workflows entered public preview June 11** — developers can now define CI/automation in natural language Markdown, compiled to Actions YAML. Combined with the GitHub Copilot SDK (now GA in 6 languages), GitHub is building the orchestration layer for the entire software development lifecycle.

- **MCP (Model Context Protocol) is becoming enterprise infrastructure.** The 2026-07-28 spec release candidate is the largest revision since launch. Google is shipping 50+ managed MCP servers. Enterprises are transitioning from MCP pilots to production at scale. Companies without an MCP strategy are accumulating integration debt.

**Recommended Attention Level: MAXIMUM — Multiple simultaneous strategic inflection points this week.**

# 2. Technologies Worth Watching

---

**Technology:** Claude Fable 5 (Anthropic Mythos-class Model)

**Category:** Artificial Intelligence — Frontier Models

**What happened?**
Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026 — the first model in its new Mythos-class tier, sitting above Opus. Priced at $10/M input tokens and $50/M output tokens (less than half the cost of previous top-tier models), it is available via API now. A companion model, Claude Mythos 5, was released exclusively for cybersecurity partners via Project Glasswing with safety guardrails lifted. Access was temporarily suspended June 12 due to unprecedented demand.

**Why it matters?**
This is the strongest model Anthropic has shipped. The temporary suspension confirms demand is outpacing compute capacity — a real supply risk for API-dependent products. The dual model strategy (safe + capability-unlocked) signals Anthropic's intent to serve both general enterprise and high-trust government/security markets.

**Business Value:** Immediate improvement in AI task quality for code generation, analysis, and agentic workflows. Lower token cost than predecessor models at higher capability.

**Company Relevance:** 90/100

**Priority:** High

**Recommendation:** Adopt

**Expected Impact:** Short

---

**Technology:** GitHub Agentic Workflows

**Category:** Developer Tools — Agentic Engineering

**What happened?**
GitHub Agentic Workflows entered public preview June 11. Teams define automations in natural language Markdown; the system compiles them to standard Actions YAML. Agents run with read-only permissions by default, inside sandboxed containers behind an Agent Workflow Firewall. Pre-built workflows cover issue triage, CI failure analysis, compliance, and documentation updates. The GitHub Copilot SDK simultaneously reached GA in Node.js, TypeScript, Python, Go, .NET, Rust, and Java — exposing the same agentic runtime that powers the Copilot app.

**Why it matters?**
This moves AI automation from the editor into the CI/CD pipeline, making agentic engineering a platform-level feature. Engineering teams can now delegate entire classes of DevOps work — triage, analysis, remediation — to agents with enterprise-grade safety controls.

**Business Value:** Significant reduction in CI/CD maintenance burden; measurable increase in engineering throughput without additional headcount.

**Company Relevance:** 88/100

**Priority:** High

**Recommendation:** Pilot

**Expected Impact:** Short

---

**Technology:** Model Context Protocol (MCP) — 2026-07-28 Specification

**Category:** Software Engineering — Interoperability Standards

**What happened?**
The 2026-07-28 MCP release candidate dropped in May — the largest spec revision since launch. Key additions: stateless HTTP core (removes session affinity requirements), MCP Apps (server-rendered UIs in sandboxed iframes), Tasks extension (long-running async jobs), OAuth 2.1/OpenID alignment, and a formal deprecation lifecycle policy. The Python and TypeScript SDKs have 97M monthly downloads. MCP was donated to the Linux Foundation (Agentic AI Foundation) in December 2025. Google launched 50+ managed MCP servers including AlloyDB Remote MCP Server (GA). Microsoft's Logic Apps MCP Server also reached GA.

**Why it matters?**
MCP is becoming the TCP/IP of agentic AI — the connective tissue between AI agents and enterprise systems. Any company building AI products without MCP compatibility is building on a proprietary island. The stateless HTTP shift makes remote MCP servers production-viable at scale.

**Business Value:** Dramatically reduces integration time for AI agents to enterprise systems; enables reuse of existing workflow investments as AI-callable tools.

**Company Relevance:** 92/100

**Priority:** High

**Recommendation:** Adopt

**Expected Impact:** Short to Medium

---

**Technology:** NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform

**Category:** Cloud & Infrastructure — AI Compute

**What happened?**
NVIDIA Vera Rubin is now ramping into full production. The platform delivers 10x agent throughput per watt vs. Grace Blackwell. Key specs: Vera Rubin NVL72 (72 Rubin GPUs + 36 Vera CPUs via NVLink 6), Groq 3 LPX Rack (35x higher inference throughput per megawatt for trillion-parameter models), Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics (co-packaged optics, now in production for million-GPU factories). The Vera CPU is purpose-built for agentic AI — 2x efficiency, 50% faster than previous-gen CPUs. Production shipments begin in H2 2026.

**Why it matters?**
The infrastructure cost per AI token is about to drop significantly. Cloud providers deploying Vera Rubin (AWS, Azure, GCP, CoreWeave, Lambda) will gain a structural cost advantage on inference. This directly affects the economics of running AI products at scale.

**Business Value:** Token cost reduction for hosted AI workloads; enables larger context windows and more concurrent agent sessions at the same price point.

**Company Relevance:** 65/100 (indirect, via cloud provider adoption)

**Priority:** Medium

**Recommendation:** Observe

**Expected Impact:** Medium

---

**Technology:** Physical AI / Humanoid Robotics — Commercial Deployment Wave

**Category:** Emerging Technology — Physical AI

**What happened?**
Three major signals this week: (1) Prometheus (Jeff Bezos, co-founded with Vik Bajaj) raised $12B at $41B valuation to build an "artificial general engineer" for physical systems. (2) NEURA Robotics closed $1.4B Series C backed by NVIDIA, Amazon, Qualcomm, Bosch, Tether. (3) Figure AI signed a commercial agreement with Catalyst Brands (JCPenney, Aéropostale, Brooks Brothers) for humanoid deployment in distribution centers. AgiBot produced its 10,000th humanoid robot in March 2026. BMW's Figure 02 robots contributed to over 30,000 vehicles.

**Why it matters?**
Physical AI is transitioning from experiment to commercial scale in 2026. Companies in manufacturing, logistics, warehousing, and supply chain face a 3–5 year window to plan their workforce-automation strategy before this becomes a competitive requirement.

**Business Value:** Long-term: labor cost reduction and 24/7 operational capacity. Short-term: operational monitoring as the industry establishes best practices and safety standards.

**Company Relevance:** 45/100

**Priority:** Medium

**Recommendation:** Observe

**Expected Impact:** Long

---

**Technology:** Cloudflare Acquires VoidZero (Vite Ecosystem)

**Category:** Developer Tools — Build Toolchain

**What happened?**
Cloudflare acquired VoidZero on June 4, bringing the entire Vite ecosystem (Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, Oxc, Vite+) under its roof. All VoidZero team members joined Cloudflare. The goal: make Vite the foundation of Cloudflare's unified CLI (`cf`), where `cf dev` becomes a superset of `vite dev`. Cloudflare plans to open-source the Void platform and create provider-agnostic primitives for full-stack apps and agents.

**Why it matters?**
Vite is already the dominant web build tool. Cloudflare now controls the build-to-deploy pipeline for a massive share of modern web applications. This consolidation will accelerate Cloudflare's position as an edge computing default — with direct implications for how we deploy frontend apps.

**Business Value:** Potential build speed improvements (Oxlint, Rolldown), unified edge deployment, and agent-compatible toolchains.

**Company Relevance:** 72/100

**Priority:** Medium

**Recommendation:** Pilot

**Expected Impact:** Medium

---

**Technology:** Supabase — Agentic Infrastructure Leader

**Category:** Developer Infrastructure — Backend-as-a-Service

**What happened?**
Supabase raised $500M Series F at $10.5B valuation (June 4), led by GIC. The company now has 9M+ developers, 250,000+ customers, and 600% database growth YoY. AI agents — primarily Claude Code and Codex — are now responsible for the majority of new databases on the platform. Supabase launched Multigres (open-source Postgres horizontal scaling), became an official ChatGPT app (29 tools), and shipped the Supabase Plugin for AI Coding Agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI).

**Why it matters?**
Supabase is the backend default for AI-native applications. Its native integration with coding agents means any team using agentic development will naturally be pulled toward Supabase. The Multigres release addresses the scaling ceiling that previously forced migration away from Postgres.

**Business Value:** Fastest path from AI-coded app to production backend; the entire infrastructure stack (DB, auth, storage, edge functions, vector search) in one platform that coding agents already know how to use.

**Company Relevance:** 85/100

**Priority:** High

**Recommendation:** Adopt

**Expected Impact:** Short

# 3. New Tools & Platforms

---

**Tool:** GitHub Agentic Workflows

**Category:** Engineering / Automation

**What does it do?**
Automates reasoning-based CI/CD tasks (issue triage, CI failure analysis, documentation updates) using AI agents defined in natural language Markdown files, compiled to standard GitHub Actions YAML. Runs in sandboxed containers with read-only defaults.

**Who should use it?**
Engineering leads, DevOps teams, platform engineering.

**Business Use Case:** Automate issue triage, failing test analysis, security scans, and documentation updates without writing YAML or hiring additional DevOps engineers.

**Estimated ROI:** Medium-high. Eliminates recurring engineering overhead on routine CI/CD tasks.

**Recommended Experiment:** Enable on one active repository. Define a natural-language workflow to automatically triage new GitHub issues into priority labels and assign to the correct team.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 1–2 weeks

---

**Tool:** GitHub Copilot SDK (GA)

**Category:** Engineering / AI

**What does it do?**
Exposes the same agentic runtime powering the GitHub Copilot app via SDK in Node.js, TypeScript, Python, Go, .NET, Rust, and Java. Enables teams to build custom AI agents (code analysis tools, release note generators, support workflow agents) on the same foundation as Copilot.

**Who should use it?**
Senior engineers, platform teams, internal tooling teams.

**Business Use Case:** Build custom internal AI agents that integrate with your codebase, issue tracker, and CI/CD without building your own LLM stack.

**Estimated ROI:** High for teams with repetitive cross-system workflows.

**Recommended Experiment:** Build one internal agent — e.g., an automated PR summary generator or changelog compiler — using the SDK in your primary language.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 2–3 weeks

---

**Tool:** agentsview (kenn-io/agentsview)

**Category:** Engineering / Productivity — AI Observability

**What does it do?**
Local-first session intelligence and analytics for coding agents. Supports Claude Code, Codex, and 20+ other agents. Billed as 100x faster replacement for ccusage. Provides visibility into agent session cost, duration, and token consumption.

**Who should use it?**
Any engineer using AI coding agents. Engineering managers tracking AI tool ROI.

**Estimated ROI:** Immediate cost visibility. Prevents runaway token spending.

**Recommended Experiment:** Install for the team's Claude Code users for one sprint. Report on average cost per PR and token efficiency.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 1 day setup, 1–2 weeks of data collection

---

**Tool:** obra/superpowers (Agentic Skills Framework)

**Category:** Engineering / AI

**What does it do?**
Open-source agentic skills framework — trending heavily on GitHub (931 stars today). Provides a modular system for equipping AI coding agents with production-grade capabilities (skills/plugins) that agents can invoke across projects.

**Who should use it?**
Technical leads building internal AI agent toolchains.

**Business Use Case:** Create a shared, version-controlled library of AI agent skills for your engineering team — reducing redundant prompt engineering and standardizing agent behavior.

**Estimated ROI:** Medium-long term multiplier: invest once, benefit across every agent session.

**Recommended Experiment:** Audit current team prompting patterns. Package the top 3 recurring prompts as reusable skills.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 2–4 weeks

---

**Tool:** IBM Langflow (via watsonx Orchestrate)

**Category:** Automation / Enterprise AI

**What does it do?**
Visual builder for AI workflows combining no-code drag-and-drop with full Python extensibility. Connects to MCP tools, LLM providers, vector databases, and internal APIs. Deployable locally, via Docker, or on IBM Cloud.

**Who should use it?**
Non-technical team members who need to build automated AI workflows; engineers building enterprise agentic pipelines.

**Business Use Case:** Build and deploy multi-step AI workflows that connect to existing enterprise systems (CRM, ERP, ticketing) without full engineering cycles.

**Estimated ROI:** High for ops-heavy workflows currently handled manually.

**Recommended Experiment:** Build one workflow automating a recurring internal reporting task using Langflow's drag-and-drop interface.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 1 week pilot

---

**Tool:** Supabase Plugin for AI Coding Agents

**Category:** Engineering / Cloud

**What does it do?**
Single-install plugin that gives AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI) complete access to Supabase capabilities: query databases, manage migrations, deploy Edge Functions, follow Postgres best practices — all without leaving the agent session.

**Who should use it?**
Any developer using AI coding agents to build database-backed applications.

**Business Use Case:** Eliminate context-switching between agent sessions and database management dashboards. Agents can handle schema migrations and Edge Function deployments autonomously.

**Estimated ROI:** Significant time savings per feature cycle for Supabase-backed projects.

**Recommended Experiment:** Install on one active project. Ask the coding agent to create and deploy a new Edge Function from scratch.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 1–2 days

---

**Tool:** MiMo Code (Xiaomi, MIT license)

**Category:** Engineering / AI Coding Agent

**What does it do?**
Open-source terminal-native AI coding agent from Xiaomi. Fork of OpenCode extended with custom memory architecture and workflow modes. Claims to outperform Claude Code on 200+ step long-horizon agentic tasks in internal benchmarks. Supports bring-your-own-model via OpenAI-compatible APIs (including DeepSeek, Kimi, or internal endpoints). Free tier with MiMo-V2.5 model access.

**Who should use it?**
Engineering teams evaluating alternatives to Claude Code, especially for very long agentic tasks or cost-sensitive environments.

**Business Use Case:** Extended autonomous coding sessions — architectural refactors, large migration tasks — where agent endurance over 200+ steps matters.

**Estimated ROI:** Low risk to evaluate given MIT license and free tier. Worth benchmark comparison.

**Recommended Experiment:** Run one matched benchmark: assign the same 200-step task to both Claude Code and MiMo Code. Compare output quality, steps taken, and token cost.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 1 week

# 4. Global Technology Direction

## Where is the market moving?

The global technology market in June 2026 is undergoing three simultaneous transitions that are compounding each other:

**Transition 1: From AI assistance to AI orchestration**
Coding assistants are becoming orchestration platforms. GitHub Copilot is now a full agentic runtime. Claude Code went from zero to the most-used AI coding tool in 8 months. Forrester's latest report confirms the SDLC is collapsing — AI agents now participate in planning, design, build, test, and delivery. The bottleneck is no longer code generation; it is governance, orchestration, and oversight.

**Transition 2: From software AI to physical AI**
Prometheus ($12B), NEURA ($1.4B), Figure (commercial deals), and Theker ($85M Europe's largest robotics Series A) all signal that the next frontier of AI is physical. The investment thesis: software AI is winner-take-most, but physical AI builds moats in the material world. Engineering simulation, humanoid labor, and autonomous manufacturing are entering commercial viability simultaneously.

**Transition 3: From experimental MCP to production MCP**
MCP has become the integration backbone for enterprise AI. The Linux Foundation governance, 97M SDK downloads, Google's 50+ managed servers, Microsoft's GA Logic Apps MCP Server, and Freshworks' production deployment signal that the protocol is no longer experimental. Enterprises that ignore MCP in 2026 will face the same technical debt that organizations faced by ignoring REST APIs in 2010.

## Major Company Movements

**Anthropic** crossed $965B valuation and $47B run-rate revenue. IPO signals are appearing (October 2026 reported). Claude Code is the most widely used AI coding tool, powering 60%+ of new Supabase databases. Fable 5 launch confirms Anthropic is pushing into government/defense via the Mythos/Glasswing track.

**Google** is executing a multi-front strategy: Gemini 3.5 Flash (shipping), Gemini 3.5 Pro (incoming, 2M context window, "Deep Think"), Gemma 4 12B (open, laptop-grade multimodal), Lightning Engine for Spark (4.9x performance), Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (Vertex AI evolution), 50+ managed MCP servers, and AlloyDB Remote MCP GA. Google is competing on breadth and infrastructure depth.

**Microsoft** launched Azure Logic Apps Automation at Build 2026 — a no-code-to-code SaaS automation platform with three AI agent integration patterns. Azure Container Apps Sandboxes provide isolated agent code execution. Microsoft is consolidating the enterprise automation stack around Azure.

**Cloudflare** acquired VoidZero to own the Vite build ecosystem. Combined with their Agents SDK (v0.14.5), Workers, D1, R2, and now the Think framework, Cloudflare is positioning as the edge-native full-stack agent deployment platform.

**NVIDIA** is shipping Vera Rubin into production H2 2026 — 10x agent throughput, purpose-built CPU, co-packaged optics for million-GPU factories. AWS committed 1M+ NVIDIA GPUs. This infrastructure wave will lower inference costs industry-wide.

## What This Means For Our Company

The window for first-mover advantage on agentic engineering is approximately 12–18 months. Companies that invest now in:
1. Agent-native CI/CD (GitHub Agentic Workflows)
2. MCP integrations for internal systems
3. AI coding agent toolchains with proper observability (agentsview, skills frameworks)

...will have compounding productivity advantages that are very difficult for laggards to replicate. The human role in software development is shifting to orchestration, quality evaluation, and strategic decomposition. Our technical leadership must redesign workflows accordingly or accept a growing productivity gap with AI-native peers.

# 6. CTO Decision Board

## START THIS WEEK

1. **Enable GitHub Agentic Workflows on 1–2 active repositories.** Define natural-language workflows for issue triage and CI failure notification. Cost is zero (included in existing GitHub plans). Upside is immediate reduction in DevOps overhead.

2. **Install agentsview for all engineers using AI coding agents.** Establish token spend baseline before scaling agent usage. This creates the evidence base for future AI tooling budget decisions.

3. **Audit which internal tools and data sources should be MCP-enabled first.** Prioritize: internal databases, ticketing systems, knowledge bases. Create a short list of the top 5 systems where AI agent access would have the highest team productivity impact.

## EVALUATE THIS MONTH

1. **Pilot Supabase as the backend for the next greenfield AI-native project.** Evaluate Multigres for projects that may need to scale beyond a single Postgres instance. The plugin for coding agents makes this a natural fit for any agentic development workflow.

2. **Run a structured benchmark of Claude Fable 5 vs. Claude Opus 4.8 on our most common AI tasks.** Fable 5 is available at no extra cost until June 22 on existing plans. Capture quality and speed data now before access requires usage credits.

3. **Evaluate the Cloudflare VoidZero acquisition impact on our build pipeline.** If you use Vite, this is a strategic supply chain signal — Cloudflare is building toward being the default deployment target for Vite apps.

## IGNORE FOR NOW

1. **Physical AI / humanoid robotics deployment planning.** The technology is real and accelerating, but enterprise deployment patterns are still forming. Revisit in Q1 2027 with a clearer market map.

2. **Recursive Superintelligence and self-improving AI.** $650M raised, 30 employees, no product shipped. High conviction bet but no practical near-term deliverable. Monitor quarterly.

3. **MiMo Code production adoption.** Evaluate for benchmarks, but do not replace primary coding agent tooling with an unproven Xiaomi-branded tool until independent third-party evaluations confirm the claimed performance gains.

# 7. Trend Detection

**Trend: Agentic coding is now the default engineering paradigm — not an experiment**
- Confidence: Very High (95%)
- Supporting signals: 22–29% adoption rate on GitHub (128K projects studied), 95% weekly AI tool usage among surveyed engineers, Claude Code becoming #1 tool in 8 months, Gartner market at $9.8–11B, Forrester confirms SDLC transformation, Anthropic publishing "2026 Agentic Coding Trends Report"
- Suggested Action: Immediately redesign engineering workflows to treat AI agents as first-class team members. Define governance, quality gates, and human oversight points for agent-generated code.

**Trend: MCP is standardizing as the enterprise AI integration layer**
- Confidence: High (87%)
- Supporting signals: 97M monthly SDK downloads, Linux Foundation governance, Google 50+ managed servers, Microsoft Logic Apps MCP Server GA, Freshworks production MCP gateway serving 700+ enterprise actions, WorkOS AuthKit MCP integration, 2026 spec release candidate arriving
- Suggested Action: Start MCP integration planning for internal systems this month. Prioritize the systems most frequently referenced in engineering and operations workflows.

**Trend: Physical AI entering commercial deployment — infrastructure investment wave**
- Confidence: High (82%)
- Supporting signals: Prometheus $12B, NEURA $1.4B, Theker $85M, Figure commercial deal, AgiBot 10K robots, BMW deployment data, NVIDIA Vera Rubin designed around agentic AI inference, Amazon expanding logistics partnerships
- Suggested Action: No immediate action required. Designate one team member to track the physical AI space monthly and report to leadership quarterly.

**Trend: AI infrastructure consolidation around NVIDIA + cloud hyperscalers**
- Confidence: Very High (93%)
- Supporting signals: AWS 1M+ NVIDIA GPUs commitment, Azure Container Apps Sandboxes, GKE standby buffers, Vera Rubin in production H2 2026, NVIDIA DSX platform standardizing AI factory design, Cloudflare Vera CPU deployment
- Suggested Action: When selecting cloud infrastructure for AI workloads, prefer providers with confirmed Vera Rubin deployment timelines. Token costs will drop materially in H2 2026–Q1 2027.

**Trend: Open-source agent skills and frameworks gaining rapid community adoption**
- Confidence: High (80%)
- Supporting signals: addyosmani/agent-skills at 57K stars (6.2K+ today), obra/superpowers at 226K stars (931 today), openclaw at 378K stars (v2026.6.6 released), awesome-claude-skills at 1,000+ production skills
- Suggested Action: Allocate 20% of AI tooling evaluation time to open-source agent skills and frameworks. Build internal skills for your most common engineering workflows.

# 8. Strategic Roadmap

## THIS MONTH (June 2026)

- **Enable GitHub Agentic Workflows** on all active repositories. Define at minimum: issue triage, CI failure notification, and security scan automation workflows.
- **Deploy agentsview** for all AI coding agent users. Establish cost-per-PR baseline and report to engineering leadership by June 30.
- **Conduct structured Claude Fable 5 vs. Opus 4.8 evaluation** before the free-access window closes June 22. Document performance differences for the 5 most common AI task types.
- **Identify top 5 internal systems for MCP integration.** Assign ownership for each integration to a named engineer.
- **Adopt Supabase** as the default backend for the next greenfield AI-native project. Verify Multigres is on the roadmap for any project expected to scale beyond 10K concurrent users.

## NEXT 90 DAYS (June–September 2026)

- **Build and ship an internal MCP server** for at least 2 high-value internal systems (e.g., internal knowledge base, ticketing system). Validate that all AI coding agents in the team can query these systems via MCP.
- **Design and publish an internal agent skills library** using the obra/superpowers or addyosmani/agent-skills model. Version-control the library and establish a contribution process.
- **Evaluate GitHub Copilot SDK** for one custom internal agent. Candidates: automated PR summary generator, changelog compiler, or internal code review assistant.
- **Reassess CI/CD pipeline** against GitHub Agentic Workflows capabilities. Identify all workflows currently requiring a human that could be delegated to an agent.
- **Evaluate Cloudflare's unified `cf` CLI** as frontend deployment target if the team uses Vite. Track VoidZero/Vite integration progress.
- **Monitor Anthropic IPO signals.** If Anthropic files for IPO in Q3/Q4 2026, prepare contingency plans for potential API pricing changes and capacity constraints.

## NEXT 12 MONTHS (June 2026–June 2027)

- **Complete MCP integration for all major internal systems.** Every internal tool used by engineering and operations should be AI-callable via MCP by Q2 2027.
- **Establish a structured agent governance framework.** Define: quality gates for agent-generated code, human review checkpoints, security scanning requirements, audit trails. This is non-negotiable at scale.
- **Evaluate physical AI relevance to company operations.** By Q1 2027, the humanoid robotics commercial landscape will be significantly clearer. Assess whether any company operations (logistics, facilities, hardware) benefit from autonomous agents in the physical world.
- **Revisit AI coding agent tooling at 6 months.** The market is moving monthly. Claude Fable 5, MiMo Code, OpenAI Codex, and open-source agents will all have significant releases. Re-evaluate the toolchain against the team's actual bottlenecks, not vendor benchmarks.
- **Assess Vera Rubin infrastructure cost impact.** When H2 2026 Vera Rubin deployments come online, renegotiate AI API pricing or evaluate direct compute options for inference-heavy workloads.
- **Consider Supabase for enterprise platform.** If building multi-tenant SaaS or AI-native products, evaluate Supabase for Platforms (370% customer growth in 6 months). The Multigres horizontal scaling layer removes the previous ceiling.

*If we only execute one thing today, it should be: **Enable GitHub Agentic Workflows on your most active repository and simultaneously audit the top 5 internal systems for MCP integration** — these two moves together begin converting your engineering team from writing automation to orchestrating it, which is the defining shift of 2026.*
